sets.passage: Artificial intelligence has quietly become one of the most (1)____ forces shaping modern life. Their growing (3)____ stems from a simple fact: machines can process volumes of data far beyond human capacity, and they can do so with remarkable speed. Yet this very power raises difficult questions. When a model makes a decision, it is often impossible to (4)____ exactly how that conclusion was reached, a problem researchers describe as the lack of transparency in so-called black-box systems. Critics warn that without proper (5)____, biased data may lead algorithms to reinforce existing inequalities rather than correct them. Companies, eager to (6)____ a competitive advantage, sometimes deploy these tools before their consequences are fully understood. Governments have responded (7)____, drafting regulations that attempt to balance innovation against the protection of individual rights. The debate has spilled beyond technical circles into ordinary conversation, as families wonder whether their jobs will survive the coming wave of automation. Meanwhile, ordinary users remain largely (8)____ of how much personal information feeds the systems they depend on daily. Experts argue that the path forward requires not only technical refinement but also a genuine commitment to ethics and sustained public education. If developers (9)____ accountability into their designs from the outset, public trust may gradually be restored. Otherwise, the gap between what technology can do and what society is willing to (10)____ will only continue to widen, leaving regulators perpetually struggling to catch up with the relentless pace of change.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇围绕人工智能的影响与监管。(1)powerful 修饰 forces；(2)determine 决定我们看什么；(3)influence 名词指影响力；(4)trace 追溯结论如何得出；(5)oversight 监督；(6)secure 取得竞争优势；(7)cautiously 谨慎地回应；(8)unaware 不知情；(9)embed 将问责嵌入设计；(10)tolerate 社会愿意容忍的限度。干扰项 abundant/neglect/swiftly/confident/dismiss 语义或语法不符。

sets.passage: Cities have always been engines of opportunity, drawing people who hope to build better lives. Yet rapid urban growth has produced challenges that planners struggle to (1)____. As populations swell, the demand for affordable housing far (2)____ the supply, forcing many residents to the distant outskirts where commutes grow longer each year. The strain extends to aging water pipes, overcrowded schools, and hospitals stretched beyond their original capacity, reminding officials that growth carries hidden costs. To address these pressures, some municipalities have begun to (4)____ heavily in public transport, hoping that reliable subways and buses will persuade commuters to abandon their cars. Others have adopted a more (5)____ vision, redesigning neighborhoods so that shops, schools, and workplaces lie within walking distance. Such compact communities, advocates claim, can dramatically reduce the (6)____ footprint of urban living while strengthening the bonds among neighbors. Critics, however, caution that without careful planning these reforms may (7)____ low-income families, pushing rents upward and displacing the very people the projects were meant to help. History offers many examples of grand schemes that beautified a district while quietly erasing the communities that once gave it life. The most successful cities tend to be those that treat residents not as passive subjects but as active (8)____ in shaping their surroundings. When local voices are genuinely (9)____ during the design process, the resulting spaces feel more humane and inclusive. Ultimately, a thriving city is less about gleaming towers than about the quiet (10)____ that allow millions of strangers to coexist with dignity, sharing streets, parks, and a common sense of belonging.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇讨论城市发展与规划。(1)overcome 克服挑战；(2)exceeds 需求超过供给；(3)annoyance 由小烦恼变折磨；(4)invest 投资公共交通；(5)ambitious 更宏大的愿景；(6)carbon 碳足迹；(7)displace 迫使低收入家庭迁离；(8)participants 居民作为参与者；(9)consulted 意见被征询；(10)arrangements 让陌生人共处的安排。干扰项 ignore/shortage/isolated/spectators/abandoned 不合语境。

sets.passage: Education has undergone a profound transformation in recent decades, yet its essential purpose remains unchanged: to help learners think for themselves. Traditional classrooms once relied on rote memorization, where students were expected to (1)____ facts and reproduce them on examinations. This approach, while efficient, often failed to (2)____ genuine curiosity, and many bright children came to regard learning as a tedious chore rather than a source of wonder. Today, many educators favor a more active model in which learners (3)____ knowledge through inquiry and discussion rather than passive listening. Technology has accelerated this shift, granting students (4)____ access to lectures, archives, and experts scattered across the globe. Online platforms allow a motivated teenager to study (5)____ alongside university material once reserved for the privileged few. Still, abundance brings its own difficulties. Faced with an overwhelming flood of information, students must (6)____ reliable sources from misleading ones, a skill that schools are only beginning to teach systematically. Without such judgment, learners can drift for hours through shallow content while believing they are making progress. Moreover, screens cannot fully (7)____ the warmth of a patient mentor who notices when a pupil is quietly struggling and offers encouragement at exactly the right moment. The most thoughtful reformers therefore advocate a balanced approach, one that (8)____ digital tools with human guidance rather than treating them as rivals. They insist that the goal is not merely to transmit content but to (9)____ the lifelong habits of disciplined reflection and intellectual honesty. When learning is framed this way, examinations cease to be the final destination and become instead a modest (10)____ along a much longer journey of intellectual growth.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇论述教育方式的转变。(1)absorb 吸收知识；(2)stimulate 激发好奇心；(3)construct 自主建构知识；(4)instant 即时获取；(5)independently 独立学习；(6)distinguish 辨别可靠来源；(7)replace 屏幕无法替代导师的温暖；(8)combines 结合数字工具与人指导；(9)cultivate 培养终身习惯；(10)milestone 考试是旅途中的里程碑。干扰项 discard/delay/reluctantly/restore/obstacle 不符语义。

sets.passage: Culture and art occupy a curious place in human society: they appear to serve no practical function, yet no civilization has ever flourished without them. A painting cannot feed the hungry, and a symphony cannot (1)____ a city, but both speak to something deeper than mere survival. Throughout history, art has served as a (2)____ in which a community examines its own values, fears, and aspirations, returning again and again to questions that science alone can never settle. When a sculptor shapes clay or a poet (3)____ a verse, the resulting work captures a fleeting moment and renders it (4)____, allowing future generations to glimpse a vanished world and to feel, however faintly, what their ancestors once felt. This is why museums and theatres are far more than entertainment; they (5)____ the collective memory of a people and bind scattered individuals into a shared inheritance. In an age dominated by commerce, however, the arts often struggle to attract adequate (6)____. Galleries close, orchestras disband, and traditional crafts (7)____ as younger generations pursue more lucrative careers in fields that promise stability and quick reward. Some governments, recognizing the danger, have moved to (8)____ cultural heritage through grants, museums, and protective legislation. Critics of such intervention argue that authentic creativity cannot be (9)____ by official decree, and that vibrant art tends to emerge from the margins rather than the comfortable centre. Whatever the mechanism, most observers agree on one point: a society that neglects its artists eventually grows spiritually impoverished, however materially (10)____ it may appear from the outside.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇讨论文化与艺术的价值。(1)defend 交响乐无法保卫城市；(2)mirror 艺术如镜子反映价值观；(3)composes 诗人创作诗句；(4)permanent 让瞬间永存；(5)preserve 保存集体记忆；(6)funding 资金支持；(7)vanish 传统工艺消失；(8)safeguard 保护文化遗产；(9)manufactured 创造力无法被官方制造；(10)prosperous 表面繁荣。干扰项 construct/window/abandons/temporary/exhausted 在语义或搭配上不唯一适配。

sets.passage: In recent decades, consumer behaviour has shifted in ways that economists struggle to (1)____ with their traditional models of rational choice. Households no longer respond to price changes in a strictly predictable manner; instead, emotional and social factors heavily (2)____ their spending decisions, sometimes in defiance of obvious self-interest. A sudden surge in disposable income, for instance, does not automatically translate into greater savings or cautious planning. On the contrary, many families treat such a windfall as a licence to (3)____ on luxury goods they had previously postponed buying for years. This tendency becomes especially (4)____ during festivals, when aggressive marketing campaigns persuade ordinary shoppers that scarcity is imminent and that delay will mean regret. Retailers deliberately exploit this anxiety by advertising limited stock and countdown timers, thereby creating an artificial sense of (5)____ that pushes hesitant buyers toward an immediate purchase. Meanwhile, the rise of instalment payment schemes has gradually (6)____ the once-firm link between owning a product and actually paying for it. Because the financial burden feels distant and conveniently fragmented, consumers (7)____ underestimate how much debt they quietly accumulate over the course of a single year. Policymakers worry that such habits may (8)____ the broader economy, since widespread borrowing leaves households dangerously vulnerable to even modest interest-rate rises. Some economists therefore advocate stricter regulation of credit advertising, arguing that genuine transparency would help citizens make more (9)____ choices. Others insist that education, rather than legislation, offers the most (10)____ remedy, because well-informed consumers naturally resist manipulative tactics and protect their own long-term financial welfare.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇讲消费心理与信贷。1 reconcile 与模型调和；2 influence 影响决策；3 splurge 挥霍买奢侈品；4 pronounced 在节日尤为明显；5 urgency 制造紧迫感；6 weakened 削弱了拥有与付款的联系；7 consistently 一贯低估债务；8 destabilise 破坏经济稳定；9 prudent 更审慎的选择；10 durable 持久的解决办法。其余 abundant/negotiate/abolish/trivial/occasionally 为干扰项。

sets.passage: Modern medicine has made remarkable progress, yet the question of how best to (1)____ chronic illness remains deeply contentious among practitioners and policymakers alike. For much of the twentieth century, physicians focused almost exclusively on treating symptoms once they had already appeared, an approach that proved both costly and frequently (2)____ over the patient's lifetime. Today, a growing body of careful research suggests that prevention is far more effective, and far cheaper, than cure. By patiently encouraging patients to (3)____ healthier lifestyles, doctors can substantially reduce the incidence of conditions such as diabetes, obesity, and heart disease. However, lasting behavioural change is notoriously difficult to (4)____, because old habits are deeply ingrained and immediate rewards usually outweigh distant, abstract benefits. Public-health campaigns therefore attempt to make healthy choices both convenient and (5)____, so that individuals adopt them almost without conscious effort. Critics argue, nonetheless, that such interventions can occasionally be (6)____, since they sometimes pressure people into decisions that ought to remain entirely personal. Despite this legitimate concern, the accumulated evidence strongly indicates that early screening can (7)____ detect dangerous abnormalities long before they become genuinely life-threatening. When a tumour is identified at an initial stage, the probability of full recovery rises (8)____, transforming a likely tragedy into a manageable inconvenience. Equally important is the role of mental well-being, which clinicians now increasingly recognise as (9)____ to physical health rather than separate from it. Integrating these dimensions allows hospitals to deliver care that is genuinely (10)____, addressing the whole person instead of a list of isolated complaints.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇讲预防医学与整体健康。1 combat 对抗慢性病；2 ineffective 旧疗法昂贵且无效；3 adopt 采纳健康生活方式；4 sustain 难以维持行为改变；5 appealing 让健康选择有吸引力；6 intrusive 干预可能太侵入私人领域；7 reliably 可靠地检出异常；8 dramatically 康复概率大幅上升；9 integral 心理健康对身体不可或缺；10 holistic 整体性照护。其余 fragile/postpone/spontaneous/negligible/decorative 为干扰项。

sets.passage: The challenge of building a sustainable future has forced both governments and corporations to (1)____ their long-standing assumptions about endless economic growth. For generations, national prosperity was measured purely by output and consumption, with remarkably little regard for the natural resources being quietly (2)____ in the process. As scientific evidence steadily mounts, however, it has become impossible for any responsible leader to (3)____ the mounting environmental costs of unchecked industrialisation. Rising global temperatures, vanishing tropical forests, and increasingly polluted oceans all testify to an economic system that is fundamentally (4)____ in its current form. Renewable energy offers one genuinely promising path forward, yet the transition is neither cheap nor (5)____ to manage in practice. Installing wind turbines and vast solar arrays requires enormous upfront investment, and the resulting power supply can prove frustratingly (6)____ whenever the weather refuses to cooperate with demand. Nevertheless, the long-term savings and the sharply reduced emissions together make such ambitious projects increasingly (7)____ from a purely economic standpoint. Beyond technology, however, genuine sustainability also demands that ordinary citizens deliberately (8)____ their daily consumption, choosing durable goods over cheap disposable ones wherever possible. Some critics caution that individual effort alone can never (9)____ a crisis of this magnitude, insisting that only coordinated international policy backed by enforceable targets will ultimately prove decisive. They point out that voluntary pledges, however well intentioned, tend to evaporate the moment economic pressures intensify and short-term profits are threatened. Even so, modest actions, when patiently multiplied across many millions of households worldwide, can together generate a (10)____ impact that gradually reshapes entire markets and pressures even the most reluctant industries to reform their wasteful practices and embrace cleaner alternatives.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇讲可持续发展与可再生能源。1 reconsider 重新考虑增长假设；2 depleted 资源被耗尽；3 ignore 无法忽视环境代价；4 unsustainable 系统不可持续；5 straightforward 转型并不简单；6 intermittent 供电间歇不稳；7 attractive 经济上更有吸引力；8 curb 抑制日常消费；9 offset 个人努力难以抵消危机规模；10 collective 集体影响。其余 abundant/reinforce/permanent/fluent/trivial 为干扰项。

sets.passage: The way information travels through society has been transformed almost beyond recognition by the rise of digital media. A century ago, news passed slowly through printed newspapers and word of mouth, allowing ample time for facts to be carefully (1)____ before they ever reached the general public. Today, by sharp contrast, a single unverified message can (2)____ across the entire globe within minutes, often long before anyone has bothered to confirm its accuracy. This unprecedented speed certainly brings obvious benefits, enabling ordinary citizens to remain genuinely (3)____ about distant events that might once have seemed wholly irrelevant to their lives. Yet the same speed also creates remarkably fertile ground for rumour and deliberate distortion. Sensational headlines are now deliberately crafted to (4)____ strong emotions, because outrage and fear reliably generate far more clicks than sober, measured analysis ever could. As a direct result, misleading stories frequently (5)____ accurate ones, effectively drowning careful reporting beneath a relentless flood of online noise. Social platforms further compound the problem through opaque algorithms that endlessly (6)____ content users already happen to agree with, gradually trapping them inside comfortable ideological bubbles. Within these enclosed spaces, genuinely opposing viewpoints rarely (7)____, and shared misconceptions slowly harden into unshakeable conviction. Responsible journalists therefore struggle to (8)____ public trust under such adverse conditions, since audiences increasingly doubt almost every source they happen to encounter. sets.explain_zh: 本篇讲数字媒体与信息传播。1 verified 旧时事实有时间核实；2 spread 消息瞬间传遍全球；3 informed 让公民了解远方事件；4 provoke 标题激起强烈情绪；5 outnumber 误导信息数量压过真实信息；6 amplify 算法放大用户认同的内容；7 surface 对立观点很少浮现；8 restore 记者努力重建公众信任；9 critically 批判性地评估证据；10 open 这是个尚无定论的开放问题。其余 concealed/diminish/fluent/decorative/negligible 为干扰项。

sets.passage: The pursuit of educational equity has become a central concern for policymakers across the globe, and few issues provoke more heated public debate. In many regions, children born into wealthy families enjoy access to well-funded schools, private tutoring, and enriching extracurricular programs, while those from disadvantaged backgrounds often (1)____ inferior resources. Such disparities are not merely a matter of (2)____; they shape the entire trajectory of a young person's life, influencing everything from career prospects to lifelong health. Researchers have repeatedly (3)____ that early exposure to quality instruction yields long-term benefits in cognitive development, social confidence, and future earnings. Yet the gap between privileged and (4)____ students continues to widen in numerous societies, despite decades of well-intentioned reform. Governments have attempted to (5)____ this imbalance through scholarships, free meals, and targeted funding programs aimed at the poorest communities. However, money alone rarely (6)____ the deeper structural problems that perpetuate inequality from one generation to the next. Teachers in remote rural areas frequently lack the training, mentorship, and support that their urban (7)____ receive almost without question. Furthermore, the (8)____ distribution of qualified educators reinforces existing inequalities rather than alleviating them, concentrating talent where it is least needed. Some reformers argue that technology could (9)____ the playing field by delivering high-quality lessons to any classroom equipped with an internet connection. Critics, on the other hand, warn that digital tools may simply (10)____ the divide if poorer schools cannot afford the necessary devices or reliable bandwidth. sets.explain_zh: 本篇围绕教育公平展开。(1)receive 接受劣质资源；(2)convenience 便利问题；(3)demonstrated 证明；(4)underprivileged 弱势学生；(5)address 解决失衡；(6)resolves 第三人称单数解决；(7)counterparts 同行；(8)uneven 不均衡分布；(9)level 拉平差距(level the playing field 固定搭配)；(10)deepen 加深鸿沟。干扰项 abandon/neutral/expensive/doubted/rapidly 在语境中均不通。

sets.passage: Cultural heritage encompasses the tangible monuments and intangible traditions that a society inherits from its ancestors and passes on to future generations. When an ancient temple is demolished to make way for a shopping center, or a traditional craft is allowed to be (3)____, the loss is permanent and ultimately affects all of humanity rather than a single nation. International organizations have therefore worked tirelessly to (4)____ endangered landmarks before they vanish forever, often racing against time and limited budgets. Such efforts frequently require enormous financial (5)____ as well as the patient cooperation of local communities who may have competing priorities and urgent daily needs. Some critics question whether scarce resources should be devoted to old stones and faded manuscripts when pressing social demands remain (6)____. Defenders respond that heritage is not a mere luxury but a (7)____ component of human identity that money can never truly replace once it disappears. They emphasize that preserving the past need not (8)____ progress; on the contrary, sensitively restored districts frequently attract tourists, generate employment, and (9)____ local economies in lasting ways. Ultimately, safeguarding our shared inheritance is a profound responsibility that transcends national borders and binds present generations to those who are yet unborn.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇主题为文化遗产。(1)link 与过去的联系；(2)threat 受到威胁(under threat)；(3)forgotten 传统被遗忘；(4)preserve 保护；(5)investment 资金投入；(6)unmet 未被满足的需求；(7)fundamental 根本性组成；(8)hinder 阻碍进步；(9)revive 振兴经济；(10)value 真正珍视。干扰项 obstacle/release/abandoned/consume/doubtful 语法或语义不合。

sets.passage: Economic globalization has woven the world's markets into a single, tightly connected web of trade, investment, and finance that few nations can afford to ignore. Proponents argue that this deepening integration has lifted hundreds of millions of people out of poverty by creating jobs and steadily lowering the (3)____ of consumer products for ordinary households. However, the benefits of this sweeping process are far from (5)____ distributed among regions, classes, and generations. sets.explain_zh: 本篇讨论经济全球化。(1)shipped 被运输；(2)instantly 几乎瞬时流动；(3)cost 降低成本；(4)profits 利润最大化；(5)evenly 均匀分配；(6)relocated 生产被转移；(7)unacceptable 难以接受的工资；(8)complexity 复杂性；(9)balance 平衡各方利益；(10)shape 塑造未来。干扰项 delayed/expense/losses/scattered/approve 在搭配上不成立。

sets.passage: In recent decades, public awareness of mental health has grown enormously, alongside a deeper scientific appreciation of how closely the mind and body (1)____ one another throughout our lives. Conversely, prolonged or serious illness frequently (3)____ a person's emotional wellbeing, creating a vicious cycle of suffering that is extremely difficult to break without help. Despite this growing understanding, many sufferers still remain (4)____ to seek treatment because of the stigma that continues to be attached to psychological disorders in so many cultures. They fear being judged as weak, unreliable, or unstable, and so they quietly (5)____ their struggles behind a carefully maintained mask of normality. Experts insist that such enforced silence is both unnecessary and deeply (6)____, since timely intervention can dramatically improve outcomes and even save lives. Simple, accessible habits, including regular exercise, adequate sleep, balanced nutrition, and meaningful social connection, can substantially (7)____ the burden of anxiety and depression for many people. sets.explain_zh: 本篇关于健康与心理。(1)influence 相互影响；(2)contribute 导致(contribute to)；(3)undermines 损害情绪；(4)reluctant 不愿求助；(5)conceal 隐藏挣扎；(6)harmful 有害的沉默；(7)ease 减轻负担；(8)responsibility 责任；(9)rise 上升；(10)routinely 通常地。干扰项 support/suffer/willing/expose/rarely 语义或搭配均不符合。

sets.passage: In an age dominated by digital connectivity, the question of personal privacy has become increasingly (1)____. Governments across the world have begun to introduce (7)____ legislation aimed at protecting ordinary citizens, but enforcement frequently lags far behind the relentless pace of technological change. sets.explain_zh: 本篇围绕科技与隐私。空1需形容词controversial（有争议的）；空2被动collected（被收集）；空3动词enhance（提升服务）；空4undermine（削弱自主权）；空5名词accuracy（准确度）；空6unaware（未察觉的）；空7形容词comprehensive（全面的立法）；空8动词exploit（利用漏洞）；空9potential（潜在后果）；空10sustained（持续的合作）。干扰项abandon/trivial/ignorant/rapidly/duration在语境或词性上均不匹配。

sets.passage: Over the past few decades, societies around the world have undergone profound (1)____ that few earlier generations could have imagined. Traditional family structures, once regarded as permanent and fixed, have gradually (2)____ to accommodate new economic and demographic realities. Widespread urbanization has drawn many millions of people away from rural villages, fundamentally (3)____ the rhythm of daily life and the customs that once governed it. As a direct result, communities that formerly relied on close personal ties and mutual support now find themselves increasingly (4)____, scattered across distant cities and unfamiliar neighbourhoods. Such dramatic shifts have created a noticeable (6)____ between generations, whose values, ambitions, and everyday expectations frequently diverge in striking ways. Modern technology has further (7)____ this transformation by radically reshaping how people communicate, socialise, and form lasting relationships. Others, by contrast, regard continuous change as an inevitable and even (9)____ force that drives genuine progress and broadens human opportunity. Historians remind us that no society has ever remained truly static for long. Whatever one's particular perspective happens to be, it is abundantly clear that the overall pace of social transformation shows no sign of (10)____, and learning to adapt to it wisely and humanely remains one of the defining challenges of our turbulent modern era.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇主题社会变迁。空1名词transformation（变革）；空2evolved（演变）；空3altering（改变节奏，动名词）；空4fragmented（破碎的）；空5名词opportunities（机会）；空6gap（代沟）；空7accelerated（加速）；空8anxiety（焦虑）；空9形容词beneficial（有益的）；空10slowing（放缓）。干扰项frequent/stable/narrowed/rejection/preserve均不合语境或词性。

sets.passage: The concept of lifelong learning has gained remarkable (1)____ in recent years, attracting the serious attention of educators, employers, and policymakers alike. No longer confined to childhood and adolescence, education is now widely understood as a (2)____ process that extends across one's entire working life and beyond. Rapid advances in technology mean that valuable skills acquired today may surprisingly quickly become (3)____, compelling thoughtful professionals to update and refine their knowledge on a continual basis. Many forward-thinking employers therefore actively (4)____ their staff to pursue further training, clearly recognizing that a well-prepared and adaptable workforce confers a decisive competitive (5)____ in crowded global markets. Online platforms have meanwhile made high-quality courses far more (6)____ than ever before, allowing motivated learners to study at their own comfortable pace regardless of location or financial background. Yet effective self-directed study demands considerable (7)____, since no attentive teacher monitors one's progress on a daily basis or gently corrects mistakes. Those who patiently (8)____ the precious habit of curiosity tend to adapt far more readily to rapidly changing circumstances and unexpected setbacks. Far from being a mere luxury reserved for the ambitious few, continuous learning has steadily become essential for genuine personal (9)____ and basic economic survival alike. Researchers note that the brain itself benefits from constant stimulation. In a world where the frontier of useful knowledge expands relentlessly with each passing year, the sustained willingness to keep learning may well prove the single most (10)____ asset that any individual can ever hope to possess.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇主题终身学习。空1名词prominence（重要性）；空2形容词continuous（持续的过程）；空3obsolete（过时的）；空4动词encourage（鼓励员工）；空5名词advantage（优势）；空6accessible（易获取的）；空7名词discipline（自律）；空8动词cultivate（培养习惯）；空9名词fulfilment（满足）；空10valuable（最宝贵的）。干扰项reluctance/temporary/discourage/scarce/neglect语义或词性不符。

sets.passage: The creative industries, encompassing diverse fields ranging from film and design to advertising, fashion, and gaming, have steadily emerged as a powerful (1)____ of many modern economies. Once carelessly dismissed as frivolous or peripheral, artistic enterprise is now widely recognized for its substantial and measurable economic (2)____. These vibrant sectors generate stable employment for many millions of workers and (3)____ significantly to national income, exports, and international prestige. Crucially, they thrive on originality and imagination, generously rewarding those bold enough to (4)____ convention and experiment with genuinely fresh ideas. Unlike traditional heavy manufacturing, creative work depends heavily on (5)____ talent and ingenuity rather than abundant physical resources or expensive machinery. Governments increasingly seek to (6)____ this enormous potential, offering targeted grants, tax incentives, and dedicated programmes to nurture emerging artists and ambitious entrepreneurs. Digital technology has further (7)____ the field in profound ways, enabling independent creators to reach vast global audiences without the backing of large studios or wealthy patrons. However, the sector also faces persistent and serious (8)____, including notoriously unstable income, irregular working hours, and fierce competition for limited attention. Protecting intellectual property remains absolutely (9)____, as widespread piracy can swiftly undermine the value of original work and discourage future investment. Cultural critics nonetheless celebrate the sheer diversity and vibrancy these dynamic industries continually produce. For nations willing to invest patiently and intelligently in their talented and imaginative citizens, the creative economy clearly offers a genuinely (10)____ path toward sustainable long-term growth, enduring cultural vitality, and a distinctive national identity admired and envied around the wider world.
sets.explain_zh: 本篇主题创意产业。空1名词engine（引擎/动力）；空2名词contribution（贡献）；空3动词contribute（贡献于收入）；空4动词defy（挑战常规）；空5形容词human（人的才能）；空6动词harness（驾驭潜力）；空7transformed（改变了领域）；空8名词challenges（挑战，复数）；空9形容词vital（至关重要的）；空10promising（有前景的）。干扰项decline/obstacle/abandon/mechanical/suppress语义或词性不匹配。
